module_exit_outcome: Learner can greet people politely, introduce themselves at a basic level, ask who/what/where questions, and survive the first minute of a conversation.
lesson_primary_outcome: Learner can greet people politely, thank others, apologize, and respond yes/no in a friendly first encounter.
lesson_secondary_outcome: Learner can recycle recent vocabulary while using basic affirmation and negation in short controlled output.
grammar_function_primary: polite particles ครับ/ค่ะ/คะ
new_vocab_core: สวัสดี = hello; ขอบคุณ = thank you; ขอโทษ = sorry / excuse me; ใช่ = yes; ไม่ใช่ = not / no; ครับ = male polite ending; ค่ะ = female polite ending
new_chunks_core: สวัสดีครับ/ค่ะ = hello; ขอบคุณครับ/ค่ะ = thank you; ไม่เป็นไร = it is okay / no problem
script_target: Recognize and read สวัสดี ขอบคุณ ขอโทษ with transliteration support.
listening_target: Catch greetings, thanks, apologies, and polite particles in slow speech.
speaking_target: Deliver a 2-line greeting exchange with the correct polite ending.
lesson_quiz_focus: Meaning recognition for greetings and correct choice of polite particles.
notes: Entry dialogue lesson. Seed the module's central real-world situation with high-utility chunks.
source_inspiration: Thai With Fah first-contact pragmatics; ThaiPod101 introduction lessons; internal backwards planning.

module_exit_outcome: Learner can greet people politely, introduce themselves at a basic level, ask who/what/where questions, and survive the first minute of a conversation.
lesson_primary_outcome: Learner can say your name, ask someone's name, and state where you are from.
lesson_secondary_outcome: Learner can recycle recent vocabulary while using origin pattern with มาจาก in short controlled output.
grammar_function_primary: name question pattern with ชื่อ
grammar_function_secondary: origin pattern with มาจาก
new_vocab_core: ชื่อ = name; คน = person; ประเทศ = country; ไทย = Thailand / Thai; อังกฤษ = England / British; อเมริกา = America; มาจาก = come from
new_chunks_core: คุณชื่ออะไร = what is your name?; ฉัน/ผมชื่อ... = my name is ...; ฉัน/ผมมาจาก... = I am from ...
review_vocab_required: สวัสดี = hello; ขอบคุณ = thank you; ขอโทษ = sorry / excuse me; ใช่ = yes
script_target: Read and match simple identity lines such as ชื่อ and ประเทศ.
listening_target: Recognize names and country statements in short self-introductions.
speaking_target: Introduce yourself with name and country in 2-3 lines.
lesson_quiz_focus: Question formation with ชื่ออะไร and origin statements with มาจาก.
notes: Primary pattern lesson. Keep output controlled and highly scaffolded.
source_inspiration: Thai With Fah first-contact pragmatics; ThaiPod101 introduction lessons; internal backwards planning.
